overview.short: Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple is the main attraction of the Shabanur village near Davangere, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple is the main attraction of the Shabanur village near Davangere, Karnataka, India. Lord Hanuman is the presiding deity of the temple.

topPlaces.name: Davanagere
topPlaces.description: Dāvanagere is a city in the centre of the southern Indian state of Karnataka. It is the seventh largest city in the state and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous Davangere district.

topPlaces.name: Shamanur
topPlaces.description: Shamanur is a village adjacent to Davanagere city and a municipal ward under the municipal corporation of Davanagere city in Davanagere district in the state of Karnataka, India. The predominantly agrarian village, nestled amidst the lush green paddy fields and towering coconut and arecanut orchards, is characterized by its suburban outlook due to its proximity to Davangere city.

topPlaces.name: Kundavada Kere
topPlaces.description: Kundavada Kere is a lake in Davanagere city, Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Kadalbal
topPlaces.description: Kadalbal (also known as KadalbaLa or Kadlabalu) is a historic town in Davanagere district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is very close to the district headquarters, Davanagere.

topPlaces.name: Pamenahalli
topPlaces.description: Pamenahalli is a village in Harihara Taluk, Karnataka, India. The total geographical area of the village is 151.66 ha (1,516,600 m2).

topPlaces.name: Doddabathi
topPlaces.description: Doddabathi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Davanagere taluk of Davanagere district.It Has A hill called Bathi gudda which is tourist place consisting a view point and Pavithra Vana Park.

items.note: Dāvanagere is a city in the centre of the southern Indian state of Karnataka. It is the seventh largest city in the state and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous Davangere district.

Shamanur is a village adjacent to Davanagere city and a municipal ward under the municipal corporation of Davanagere city in Davanagere district in the state of Karnataka, India. The predominantly agrarian village, nestled amidst the lush green paddy fields and towering coconut and arecanut orchards, is characterized by its suburban outlook due to its proximity to Davangere city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (129 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (129 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Shamanur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Davanagere, Shamanur, Kundavada Kere, Kadalbal, Pamenahalli.
